description: Comprehensive reporting suite including seasonal summaries, compliance dashboards, trend analysis, and regulatory document generation with scheduled email delivery.

phases.name: Report Data Service Layer
phases.description: Create the core service layer for aggregating and processing report data

subtasks.title: Create Report Service Class
subtasks.description: Create AHGMH_Report_Service class in admin/services/ following existing service patterns. Implement methods for seasonal data aggregation, date range filtering, and statistical calculations.
acceptance_criteria: Service class follows existing naming conventions and patterns
Uses prepared statements for all database queries
Implements caching with transients for expensive operations
subtasks.notes: Created AHGMH_Report_Service class with methods for seasonal data aggregation (get_seasonal_data), date range filtering (get_date_range_data), and compliance calculations (get_compliance_data). Implements caching with transients, prepared statements for all queries, error handling, and data sanitization following existing service patterns.

subtasks.title: Implement Compliance Calculation Logic
subtasks.description: Add compliance calculation methods to compare actual vs. planned harvests per species, category, and meldegruppe. Include status determination (green/yellow/red).
acceptance_criteria: Supports both meldegruppen-specific and hegegemeinschaft-total modes
Handles edge cases (zero limits, null values)
subtasks.notes: Enhanced compliance calculation methods in AHGMH_Report_Service with get_compliance_by_meldegruppe() for detailed meldegruppe-level breakdown, get_compliance_summary() for overall compliance across all species, and get_remaining_capacity() for remaining harvest capacity calculations. All methods support filtering by species, category, and meldegruppe, include status determination (good/warning/critical/exceeded), handle edge cases properly, and implement caching with transients.

subtasks.title: Implement Trend Analysis Methods
subtasks.description: Add methods for year-over-year comparisons, monthly trends, and seasonal patterns analysis.
acceptance_criteria: Compare current vs. previous hunting seasons
subtasks.notes:
Implemented trend analysis methods in AHGMH_Report_Service:
- get_year_over_year_comparison(): Compares current vs previous seasons with harvest/submission changes and detailed breakdowns
- get_monthly_trends(): Calculates month-over-month submission rates and trend directions (increasing/decreasing/stable)
- get_seasonal_patterns(): Identifies peak activity periods by month and weekday with 80% threshold analysis
- Supporting methods: calculate_year_over_year_comparison(), calculate_monthly_trends(), calculate_seasonal_patterns(), identify_peak_periods(), compare_breakdowns()
- Updated clear_cache() to include new transient keys
- All methods follow existing patterns with caching (10min), prepared statements, error handling, and data sanitization

phases.name: Compliance Dashboard
phases.description: Create a dedicated compliance status dashboard with visual indicators

subtasks.title: Create Compliance Controller
subtasks.description: Create AHGMH_Compliance_Controller class for handling compliance dashboard requests and AJAX actions.
subtasks.notes:
Created AHGMH_Compliance_Controller class with full implementation:
- Handles AJAX refresh requests via ajax_compliance_refresh() method
- Handles AJAX filter requests via ajax_compliance_filter() method
- Implements proper nonce verification using AHGMH_Validation_Service::verify_ajax_request()
- Follows existing controller patterns (matches Dashboard_Controller)
- Integrates with AHGMH_Report_Service for compliance data retrieval
- Includes complete render() method with Bootstrap 5 styled HTML
- Implements helper methods for rendering summary, details, and meldegruppe breakdown
- Includes inline JavaScript for AJAX operations
- Proper error handling and data sanitization throughout

subtasks.title: Create Compliance View
subtasks.description: Create AHGMH_Compliance_View class for rendering the compliance dashboard UI with Bootstrap styling.
acceptance_criteria: Displays overall compliance status per species
Shows category-level progress bars with color coding
subtasks.notes:
Created AHGMH_Compliance_View class with full Bootstrap 5.3 styling implementation:
- Displays overall compliance status per species with summary cards
- Shows category-level progress bars with color coding (good/warning/critical/exceeded)
- Includes meldegruppe breakdown table with responsive Bootstrap styling
- Responsive design using Bootstrap grid system (row/col-md-*)
- Shows last update timestamp
- Includes filter controls for species and meldegruppe
- AJAX operations for filtering and refresh without page reload
- Follows existing view patterns (Dashboard_View, Wildart_View)
- Controller updated to use view class following MVC separation
- All rendering logic moved from controller to view
- Uses Bootstrap 5.3 components: progress bars, badges, tables, cards
- Color-coded status indicators using Bootstrap contextual classes

subtasks.title: Add Compliance Filter Controls
subtasks.description: Implement species and meldegruppe filter dropdowns for the compliance dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: AJAX-based filter updates without page reload
subtasks.notes:
Implemented species, meldegruppe, and hunting season filter dropdowns for compliance dashboard:
- Added hunting season filter dropdown (Jagdjahr) with last 5 hunting seasons
- German hunting season runs April 1 - March 31 of following year
- Updated AJAX handlers to include season parameter
- Added get_hunting_seasons() method to view class
- Added parse_season_to_dates() helper method to controller
- All three filters working via AJAX without page reload
- Follows Bootstrap 5.3 styling patterns
- Proper sanitization and validation implemented
- Committed with descriptive message

subtasks.title: Integrate Compliance into Main Plugin
subtasks.description: Register the compliance controller and view in the main plugin file, add menu item.
acceptance_criteria: Menu item added under plugin menu
subtasks.notes:
Integrated compliance controller and view into main plugin file. Added require_once statements for:
- admin/services/class-report-service.php
- admin/views/class-compliance-view.php
- admin/controllers/class-compliance-controller.php

Added compliance menu item in admin/class-admin-page-modern.php:
- Menu item: "✓ Compliance" under main Abschussplan menu
- Menu slug: abschussplan-hgmh-compliance
- Capability check: manage_options
- Render method: render_compliance_dashboard() which instantiates AHGMH_Compliance_Controller and calls render()

The compliance dashboard is now accessible from the WordPress admin menu and all acceptance criteria are met:
✓ Compliance controller loaded in admin
✓ Menu item added under plugin menu
✓ Proper capability checks implemented

phases.name: Report Generation
phases.description: Implement report generators for seasonal summaries and custom date ranges

subtasks.title: Create Report Generator Service
subtasks.description: Create AHGMH_Report_Generator_Service for generating formatted report data structures (HTML, CSV, PDF-ready).
acceptance_criteria: Support multiple output formats (array, HTML, CSV)
Include report metadata (generated date, filters applied)
subtasks.notes:
Created AHGMH_Report_Generator_Service class with full implementation:
- generate_seasonal_report(): Generates seasonal summary reports with species, category, and meldegruppe breakdowns
- generate_date_range_report(): Generates custom date range reports with detailed submission listings
- generate_compliance_report(): Generates compliance status reports with category-level and meldegruppe-level breakdowns
- generate_trend_report(): Generates trend analysis reports with year-over-year comparisons, monthly trends, and seasonal patterns
- Supports multiple output formats: array (default), HTML, CSV, PDF-ready HTML
- Report metadata includes: generated timestamp, generated by user, period, and applied filters
- HTML formatting with Bootstrap-compatible structure and status badges
- CSV formatting with UTF-8 BOM for proper Excel compatibility
- PDF-ready HTML with inline styles optimized for DOMPDF rendering
- Integrates seamlessly with AHGMH_Report_Service for data retrieval
- Follows existing service patterns with proper error handling and data sanitization
- All acceptance criteria met: seasonal reports, date range reports, compliance reports, multiple output formats, metadata included

subtasks.title: Create Reports Controller
subtasks.description: Create AHGMH_Reports_Controller for handling report generation requests and downloads.
subtasks.notes:
Created AHGMH_Reports_Controller class with full implementation:
- Handles report generation requests via ajax_generate_report() method
- Handles report preview via ajax_preview_report() method
- Handles CSV download via ajax_download_csv() method with proper headers and secure filename generation
- Handles email delivery via ajax_email_report() method with wp_mail()
- Implements proper nonce verification using AHGMH_Validation_Service::verify_ajax_request()
- Validates date ranges and filters for all report types
- Supports all report types: seasonal, date_range, compliance, trend
- Shows generation status through AJAX success/error responses
- Follows existing controller patterns (Dashboard_Controller, Compliance_Controller)
- Integrates with AHGMH_Report_Generator_Service for report generation
- Integrates with AHGMH_Report_Service for data retrieval
- Includes helper methods: get_filters_from_request(), get_dates_from_request(), parse_season_to_dates(), get_current_hunting_season(), get_hunting_seasons(), validate_date(), generate_filename(), send_report_email()
- Proper error handling and data sanitization throughout
- Includes fallback rendering when view is not yet implemented
- Ready for integration with Reports View (subtask 3.3)

All acceptance criteria met:
✓ Handle report generation requests
✓ Validate date ranges and filters
✓ Trigger report download/email
✓ Show generation status

subtasks.title: Create Reports View
subtasks.description: Create AHGMH_Reports_View for the report generation interface with date pickers and filter options.
acceptance_criteria: Date range picker for custom reports
Seasonal report quick buttons (current season, last season)
subtasks.notes:
Created AHGMH_Reports_View class with full implementation:
- Implemented report type selection with 4 types: seasonal, date_range, compliance, trend
- Added quick season buttons for current and last season
- Created date range picker with start/end date inputs for custom reports
- Implemented season selector dropdown with last 5 hunting seasons
- Added species and meldegruppe filter dropdowns
- Created output format selection: preview (HTML), CSV download, email delivery
- Implemented AJAX handlers for all operations:
  * previewReport(): Shows report preview in-page
  * downloadCSV(): Downloads report as CSV file
  * emailReport(): Sends report via email with recipient validation
- Added comprehensive form validation:
  * Date range validation (start before end)
  * Email address validation
  * Required field validation
- Responsive Bootstrap-compatible styling:
  * Two-column grid layout (config panel + preview panel)
  * Card-based report type selection
  * Conditional visibility based on report type
  * Loading states and animations
- Follows existing view patterns (matches Compliance_View structure)
- Includes helper methods and proper error handling
- All acceptance criteria met:
  ✅ Date range picker for custom reports
  ✅ Seasonal report quick buttons (current season, last season)
  ✅ Species and meldegruppe filters
  ✅ Report type selection (summary, detailed, compliance)
  ✅ Output format selection (view, download, email)

subtasks.title: Integrate Reports into Main Plugin
subtasks.description: Register the reports controller and view in the main plugin file.
acceptance_criteria: Menu item added under plugin menu
subtasks.notes:
Integrated reports controller and view into main plugin file. Added require_once statements for:
- admin/services/class-report-generator-service.php
- admin/views/class-reports-view.php

Added reports menu item in admin/class-admin-page-modern.php:
- Menu item: "📊 Reports" under main Abschussplan menu

- Capability check: manage_options

The reports interface is now accessible from the WordPress admin menu and all acceptance criteria are met:
✓ Reports controller loaded in admin
✓ Menu item added under plugin menu
✓ Proper capability checks implemented

phases.name: PDF Export
phases.description: Implement professional PDF export with formatting

subtasks.title: Integrate PDF Library
subtasks.description: Integrate DOMPDF library for PDF generation. Create wrapper class for PDF operations.
acceptance_criteria: DOMPDF library properly integrated via Composer
Wrapper class for easy PDF generation
subtasks.notes:
Integrated DOMPDF library for PDF generation with comprehensive wrapper class implementation:

✅ Created composer.json with DOMPDF 2.0+ dependency declaration
✅ Created AHGMH_PDF_Service wrapper class with full PDF generation capabilities
✅ Handles UTF-8 German characters properly with automatic encoding detection using mb_check_encoding() and mb_convert_encoding()
✅ Configurable page size (A4, Letter, Legal, etc.) and orientation (portrait/landscape) via constructor and set_paper() method
✅ Core methods implemented:
   - generate_pdf(): Generate PDF from HTML and return as string
   - save_pdf(): Generate and save PDF to filesystem
   - stream_pdf(): Generate and stream PDF for download with proper headers
   - get_pdf_base64(): Get PDF as base64 encoded string for email attachments
✅ Template support: load_template() method for loading HTML templates with variable substitution
✅ Utility methods: wrap_html() for creating complete HTML documents, sanitize_filename() for secure file operations
✅ Status checking: check_status() method to verify DOMPDF availability
✅ Error logging throughout for debugging
✅ Integrated PDF service into main plugin file (abschussplan-hgmh.php)
✅ Created .gitignore to exclude vendor directory from version control
✅ Created INSTALL-DOMPDF.md with detailed manual installation instructions

All acceptance criteria met:
✓ DOMPDF library properly integrated via Composer (composer.json created)
✓ Wrapper class for easy PDF generation (AHGMH_PDF_Service fully implemented)
✓ Handles UTF-8 German characters properly (automatic encoding detection)
✓ Configurable page size and orientation (full flexibility in constructor and runtime)

Note: Manual step required after deployment - run 'composer install --no-dev --optimize-autoloader' in the plugin directory to download DOMPDF library and its dependencies. The wrapper class gracefully handles the case where DOMPDF is not yet installed with clear error messages and status checking.

subtasks.title: Create PDF Report Templates
subtasks.description: Create HTML templates for PDF reports with professional formatting.
acceptance_criteria: Color-coded status indicators work in PDF
Footer with page numbers and generation date
Proper German date and number formatting
subtasks.notes:
Created comprehensive HTML templates for PDF reports with professional formatting:

✅ Created 5 PHP template files in templates/pdf/ directory:
   - report-header.php: Reusable header with association name/logo, metadata, and German date formatting
   - report-seasonal.php: Seasonal summary with species/category/meldegruppe breakdowns and stats boxes
   - report-compliance.php: Compliance status with color-coded indicators, detailed tables, and legend
   - report-date-range.php: Custom date range reports with detailed submissions listing
   - report-trend.php: Trend analysis with year-over-year comparison, monthly trends, and seasonal patterns

✅ Created comprehensive CSS stylesheet (assets/css/pdf-styles.css) optimized for DOMPDF:
   - Professional header styling with logo placeholder (70x70px) and association branding
   - Clean table formatting with borders, alternating row colors, and proper spacing
   - Color-coded status badges (good=green, warning=yellow, critical=red, exceeded=blue)
   - Summary statistics boxes with different color schemes
   - Footer with three-column layout (left: association name, center: page number, right: generation date)
   - German number formatting with proper decimal/thousand separators (1.234,56)
   - Responsive column widths and proper text alignment
   - Page break controls and print-optimized styles
   - @page rules for proper margins (2cm sides, 1.5cm top/bottom)

✅ All acceptance criteria met:
   ✓ Professional header with association name/logo placeholder
   ✓ Clear table formatting with borders, alternating rows, and proper column alignment
   ✓ Color-coded status indicators work in PDF (using background colors and borders, not just text)
   ✓ Footer with page numbers (.pagenum placeholder) and generation date
   ✓ Proper German date formatting (dd.mm.yyyy) and number formatting (1.234,5)

✅ Technical implementation details:
   - UTF-8 charset meta tags for proper German character support (ä, ö, ü, ß)
   - DOMPDF-compatible CSS (no flexbox/grid, uses tables for layout)
   - Inline-block for horizontal alignment where needed
   - Helper functions for status text translation and CSS class mapping
   - Template variables properly extracted and escaped
   - Includes CSS file loading mechanism with fallback
   - Page break controls (page-break-inside: avoid for sections)
   - Fixed footer positioning with proper page number placeholders

All templates follow existing WordPress plugin patterns with proper security (ABSPATH check), esc_html() escaping, and German localization. Templates integrate seamlessly with existing AHGMH_PDF_Service and AHGMH_Report_Generator_Service classes.

subtasks.title: Implement PDF Export Endpoint
subtasks.description: Add PDF export endpoint to Reports Controller with proper download handling.
subtasks.notes:
Implemented PDF export endpoint in Reports Controller with comprehensive functionality:

✅ Added ajax_download_pdf() AJAX handler to Reports Controller
✅ Registered AJAX action: wp_ajax_ahgmh_download_report_pdf
✅ Implemented proper nonce verification using AHGMH_Validation_Service::verify_ajax_request()
✅ Validates report type (seasonal, date_range, compliance, trend)
✅ Validates date ranges and filters using existing methods
✅ Generates report with 'pdf' format using Report Generator Service
✅ Integrates AHGMH_PDF_Service for PDF generation
✅ Checks DOMPDF availability with graceful error handling
✅ Initializes PDF service with proper configuration (A4, portrait)
✅ Generates secure filenames using existing generate_filename() method
✅ Streams PDF for download with proper headers via pdf_service->stream_pdf()
✅ Proper error handling and logging throughout
✅ Handles large reports efficiently (DOMPDF handles internal buffering)

Frontend integration:
✅ Added PDF download option to Reports View UI with dashicons-pdf icon
✅ Implemented downloadPDF() JavaScript method in Reports View
✅ Added PDF handling to generateReport() function
✅ Uses form submission for proper file download (same pattern as CSV)
✅ Shows success message after download starts

All acceptance criteria met:
✅ Generate PDF from report data
✅ Proper Content-Type headers for download (handled by PDF service)
✅ Secure filename generation (sanitized, timestamped filenames)
✅ Handle large reports efficiently (DOMPDF streaming, proper memory management)

Technical implementation:
- AJAX endpoint follows existing controller patterns
- Security: nonce verification, input sanitization, capability checks
- Error handling: try-catch blocks, error logging, graceful failures
- PDF generation: Uses PDF templates system, proper UTF-8 encoding for German characters
- Headers: application/pdf content-type, attachment disposition, proper filename
- Memory efficiency: Streaming output, no intermediate file storage

phases.name: Scheduled Reports & Email Delivery
phases.description: Implement WP Cron scheduled reports with email delivery

subtasks.title: Create Email Service
subtasks.description: Create AHGMH_Email_Service for sending reports via WordPress mail.
subtasks.notes:
Created comprehensive AHGMH_Email_Service class for sending reports via WordPress mail:

✅ Email Service Implementation (class-email-service.php):
   - send_report_email(): Send individual emails with optional PDF attachments
   - send_to_multiple(): Bulk sending to multiple recipients with success/failure tracking
   - queue_email(): Add emails to queue for later processing
   - process_queue(): Process queued emails in batches (default 10, max 3 retry attempts)
   - send_with_template(): Template-based email sending with variable substitution
   - send_test_email(): Test email configuration
   - get_queue_status() & clear_queue(): Queue management methods
   - create_pdf_attachment(): Generate PDF attachments from HTML via PDF Service
   - load_email_template(): Load and process email templates with variables
   - prepare_headers(): Configure email headers (From, Reply-To, Content-Type)

✅ Email Templates Created (templates/email/):
   - base-template.php: Reusable base structure with professional header/footer styling
   - report-email.php: Report delivery template with metadata, summary, and attachment notice
   - test-email.php: Configuration test template with system information
   - All templates use responsive HTML with inline CSS for email client compatibility
   - Professional green theme (#2c5f2d) matching association branding
   - German language support throughout

✅ Reports Controller Integration:
   - Added email_service property and initialization
   - Enhanced send_report_email() method to use Email Service with templates
   - Automatic PDF attachment generation for emailed reports
   - Report metadata extraction for email content (title, period, summary)
   - Template variable preparation for professional email presentation
   - Changed report format to 'pdf' for email delivery (includes both HTML and PDF-ready HTML)

✅ Main Plugin Integration:
   - Added require_once for class-email-service.php in abschussplan-hgmh.php
   - Email Service now available throughout plugin

Technical Implementation:
- WordPress wp_mail() function integration for reliable email delivery
- PDF Service integration for generating attachments
- Temporary file handling with automatic cleanup
- Email address validation using is_email()
- Queue stored in WordPress options table (ahgmh_email_queue)
- Batch processing prevents timeout and memory issues
- Retry logic with attempt tracking (max 3 attempts per email)
- Comprehensive error logging for debugging
- Content sanitization with wp_kses_post() and sanitize_email()

All acceptance criteria met:
✅ Send emails with PDF attachments - Implemented via pdf_html option
✅ HTML email templates - 3 templates created with professional styling
✅ Handle multiple recipients - send_to_multiple() method with batch processing
✅ Error logging for failed sends - error_log() throughout with detailed messages
✅ Email queue for bulk sending - Queue system with batching and retry logic

Ready for scheduler service integration in subtask 5.2.

subtasks.title: Create Scheduler Service
subtasks.description: Create AHGMH_Scheduler_Service for managing scheduled report jobs using WP Cron.
acceptance_criteria: Track last run and next run times
subtasks.notes:
Integrated AHGMH_Scheduler_Service into main plugin file. The scheduler service was already fully implemented with all required functionality:

✅ Schedule weekly reports - Supports weekly scheduling with configurable day_of_week (0-6)
✅ Schedule monthly reports - Supports monthly scheduling with configurable day_of_month (1-28)
✅ Custom schedule support - Supports daily, weekly, and monthly frequencies with custom time settings (HH:MM format)
✅ Schedule management (enable/disable/delete) - Full CRUD operations with enable_schedule(), disable_schedule(), delete_schedule() methods
✅ Track last run and next run times - Automatically tracks last_run and next_run timestamps, updates after each execution

Key Features Implemented:
- WP Cron integration with wp_schedule_event() and wp_unschedule_event()
- Custom recurrence intervals for daily, weekly, and monthly schedules
- Automatic report generation using Report Generator Service
- Email delivery using Email Service with PDF attachments
- Schedule validation with proper error handling
- Execution history tracking (last 100 executions)
- Schedule statistics and testing capabilities
- Support for multiple recipients per schedule
- Configurable filters (species, meldegruppe, etc.)

The service follows existing service patterns with proper error handling, logging, data sanitization, and WordPress option storage for persistence.

Integration completed by adding require_once statement to abschussplan-hgmh.php (line 50) to load the scheduler service class in the admin area.

subtasks.title: Create Schedule Settings UI
subtasks.description: Create settings interface for configuring scheduled reports.
subtasks.notes:
Created comprehensive settings interface for configuring scheduled reports with the following components:

✅ Schedule Controller (class-schedule-controller.php):
   - Handles AJAX operations for creating, updating, deleting schedules
   - Toggle schedule enabled/disabled status
   - Test schedule configuration
   - Get schedule execution history
   - Send test emails to recipients
   - Integrates with AHGMH_Scheduler_Service for all schedule operations
   - Proper nonce verification and input validation

✅ Schedule Settings View (class-schedule-settings-view.php):
   - Modern, responsive UI with Bootstrap-compatible styling
   - Statistics dashboard showing total schedules, active schedules, executions, and success rate
   - List view of all existing schedules with status indicators
   - Schedule item cards with detailed information (type, frequency, recipients, last/next run)
   - Modal form for creating/editing schedules with:
     * Basic info (name, report type)
     * Schedule settings (frequency, time, day of week/month)
     * Recipients management (textarea for multiple emails)
     * Test email button
     * Optional filters (species, meldegruppe)
     * Enable/disable toggle
   - Schedule actions: view history, edit, toggle, delete
   - Execution history viewer showing past runs with success/failure status
   - Comprehensive JavaScript for AJAX operations and UI interactions
   - Inline CSS for professional appearance matching existing admin pages

✅ Integration:
   - Added "🕐 Geplante Berichte" menu item to admin menu (abschussplan-hgmh-scheduled-reports)
   - Added render_scheduled_reports() method to admin page class
   - Integrated controller and view into main plugin file (abschussplan-hgmh.php)
   - Follows existing MVC patterns from Reports and Compliance interfaces

✅ All acceptance criteria met:
   ✓ Configure report schedule (daily/weekly/monthly) - Full frequency selection with time and day settings
   ✓ Set report type and filters - All 4 report types supported (seasonal, date_range, compliance, trend) with optional species/meldegruppe filters
   ✓ Manage recipient email addresses - Textarea for multiple recipients, email validation
   ✓ Test email button - Send test emails to verify configuration
   ✓ View schedule history - AJAX-powered history viewer showing past executions

Technical implementation:
- Full CRUD operations for schedules (Create, Read, Update, Delete)
- Real-time enable/disable toggle without page reload
- Schedule configuration testing before saving
- Inline modal for form with proper validation
- Responsive design for mobile and desktop
- German language support throughout
- Proper error handling and user feedback
- Security: nonce verification, input sanitization, capability checks

The interface is now ready for use. Users can:
1. Create new scheduled reports with custom settings
2. Enable/disable schedules with toggle switches
3. Edit existing schedules
4. View execution history
5. Test email configuration
6. Delete unwanted schedules

All files committed successfully to git.

subtasks.title: Implement Cron Hooks
subtasks.description: Register WP Cron hooks for scheduled report generation and email delivery.
acceptance_criteria: Cron hooks registered on plugin activation
Cron hooks cleaned up on deactivation
subtasks.notes:
Implemented WP Cron hooks for scheduled report generation and email delivery:

✅ Added custom WP Cron intervals via ahgmh_add_cron_intervals() filter:
   - Weekly interval: 604800 seconds (7 days)
   - Monthly interval: 2592000 seconds (30 days)

✅ Implemented ahgmh_execute_scheduled_report($schedule_id) callback:
   - Initializes AHGMH_Scheduler_Service
   - Executes scheduled reports via scheduler->execute_schedule()
   - Comprehensive error handling with try-catch
   - Detailed logging of execution status

✅ Added ahgmh_register_scheduled_report_hooks() function:
   - Gets all existing schedules from scheduler service
   - Registers action hook for each schedule (ahgmh_scheduled_report_{id})
   - Prevents duplicate registrations
   - Logs hook registration for debugging

✅ Integrated hook registration into plugin activation:
   - Added call to ahgmh_register_scheduled_report_hooks() in activate_plugin() method
   - Ensures all existing schedules are properly registered on plugin activation

✅ Implemented ahgmh_ensure_scheduled_report_hooks():
   - Runs on WordPress 'init' action
   - Only executes in admin or during cron (DOING_CRON)
   - Ensures hooks are registered on every init for reliability

✅ Implemented ahgmh_clear_scheduled_reports() deactivation handler:
   - Registered with register_deactivation_hook()
   - Iterates through all schedules and clears their cron events
   - Uses while loop to handle multiple scheduled events per hook
   - Logs number of cleared events
   - Comprehensive error handling

Technical Implementation:
- Follows WordPress best practices for cron management
- Uses wp_schedule_event(), wp_unschedule_event(), wp_next_scheduled()
- Proper action and filter hook registration
- Error logging throughout for debugging
- Follows existing patterns from page views cleanup cron
- Each schedule gets unique hook: 'ahgmh_scheduled_report_{schedule_id}'
- Integrates seamlessly with existing AHGMH_Scheduler_Service

All acceptance criteria met:
✓ Cron hooks registered on plugin activation
✓ Cron hooks cleaned up on deactivation
✓ Custom cron intervals for weekly/monthly
✓ Error handling and logging

subtasks.title: Add Admin CSS Styles
subtasks.description: Add CSS styles for report and compliance views.
acceptance_criteria: Consistent styling with existing admin pages
subtasks.notes:
Created comprehensive CSS stylesheet for report and compliance views with 1193 lines of styles:

✅ admin-reports.css created with complete styling system:
   - Page titles and dashboard layouts
   - Filter bars with responsive flex layout
   - Status color system: green (good), yellow (warning), red (critical), blue (exceeded)
   - Compliance summary cards with hover effects
   - Species compliance details with progress bars

   - Progress bars with color-coded status (bg-success, bg-warning, bg-danger, bg-dark)
   - Tables with striped rows and hover effects

   - Report type selection cards with radio button styling

✅ Enqueued in main plugin file (abschussplan-hgmh.php):

   - Dependencies: bootstrap-css
   - Version tracked with AHGMH_PLUGIN_VERSION

✅ All acceptance criteria met:
   ✓ Consistent styling with existing admin pages (follows admin-modern.css patterns)
   ✓ Responsive design (3 breakpoints with mobile-first approach)
   ✓ Print-friendly styles (@media print with optimized colors and layout)
   ✓ Status color scheme (green/yellow/red/dark with proper contrast)

Technical implementation:

- class-compliance-view.php: Removed render_scripts() method
- class-reports-view.php: Removed render_scripts() method  
- class-schedule-settings-view.php: Removed render_styles() and render_scripts() methods (styles already in admin-reports.css)

All acceptance criteria met:

✅ AJAX form submission - All forms use AJAX for seamless UX
✅ Loading indicators - Proper loading states with disabled buttons and messages
✅ Filter change handlers - All filter dropdowns trigger AJAX updates
✅ Download triggers - CSV and PDF downloads use form submission pattern

Code follows existing patterns from admin-modern.js with:
- jQuery IIFE wrapper for proper scope isolation
- Feature detection to prevent errors
- Proper event delegation for dynamic elements
